channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Gwanda Magistrate Exposes ‘Zvigananda’ in Drug Trade: Who’s Protecting these Kingpins?
item.description: In a powerful and unprecedented move, Gwanda Magistrate Wayne Moyo has cast a stark light on the shadowy figures allegedly orchestrating Zimbabwe’s burgeoning drug trade. His recent pronouncements suggest that politically-connected individuals, colloquially known as ‘Zvigananda’, are operating with alarming impunity, while the legal system disproportionately targets vulnerable drug users. This revelation, made during the [...] The post Gwanda Magistrate Exposes ‘Zvigananda’ in Drug Trade: Who’s Protecting these Kingpins? first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Amazon will launch its satellite internet in South Africa, seemingly beating Musk in his homeland
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa — Amazon said Wednesday that the technology company will launch its new satellite internet service Amazon Leo in South Africa in 2027, seemingly pushing ahead of Elon Musk’s rival Starlink to win business in Africa’s most advanced economy. Amazon, which was founded by Jeff Bezos, said that it would partner with [...] The post Amazon will launch its satellite internet in South Africa, seemingly beating Musk in his homeland appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: China grew at its slowest pace in more than 3 years last quarter
item.description: HONG KONG — China’s economy slowed sharply to a 4.3% annualized pace of growth in the April-June quarter, the government said Wednesday, the weakest in over three years. The official data fell short of forecasts and was far below the economy’s strong 5% pace of growth in January-March, despite a surge in exports driven partly [...] The post China grew at its slowest pace in more than 3 years last quarter appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: FBI Nudged to Investigate The US$1.5 Million Mapfumo–Chivayo Deal
item.description: By A Correspondent | A Zimbabwean-born UK citizen has formally written to several United States law enforcement and financial oversight... Continue reading "FBI Nudged to Investigate The US$1.5 Million Mapfumo–Chivayo Deal" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe prepares to receive up to 1.4 million land owners from SA @bulawayo24
item.description: Zimbabwe has more than two million citizens living in South Africa, but fewer than 100,000 have so far returned home under the government’s repatriation programme, despite rising anti-immigrant tensio... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: IMF expects world economy to grow a sluggish 3% this year, weighed down by Iran war but helped by AI
item.description: WASHINGTON — The International Monetary Fund on Wednesday modestly downgraded its outlook for the world economy this year, citing the energy shock caused by the Iran war. But the fallout from the conflict is being partially offset by booming investment in artificial intelligence and other technologies. The IMF now expects the global economy to expand [...] The post IMF expects world economy to grow a sluggish 3% this year, weighed down by Iran war but helped by AI appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Spain shuts down France and Kylian Mbappé, advances to the World Cup final with a 2-0 victory
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas — Spain’s defensive prowess and swagger were just too much for an attacking trio led by France great Kylian Mbappé, and just enough to get the 2010 champions into another World Cup final. The Spanish team managed a record sixth shutout in seven games so far, winning 2-0 in the semifinals Tuesday against [...] The post Spain shuts down France and Kylian Mbappé, advances to the World Cup final with a 2-0 victory appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Mapfumo’s Chivayo deal sparks debate @bulawayo24 @wicknellchivayo
item.description: Zimbabwe’s heated debate over Thomas "Mukanya" Mapfumo’s decision to accept controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo’s reported US$1 million performance package has intensified, with family members,... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: CCC MPs allege assault by suspected CIOs in Mutare @bulawayo24
item.description: Two Citizens Coalition for Change (CCC) Members of Parliament have alleged that they were assaulted by suspected Central Intelligence Organisation (CIO) operatives following a dispute over a parking b... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Old Mutual’s OMARI Brings Winter Warmth to Vulnerable Children @263Chat
item.description: By Tinomudaishe Muzanenhamo Old Mutual Zimbabwe, through its digital financial services platform OMARI has donated 100 blankets to children at Matthew Rusike Children’s Home as part of its 2026 Winter Warmer Campaign in a bid to protect vulnerable children from the harsh winter conditions. The [...] The post Old Mutual’s OMARI Brings Winter Warmth to Vulnerable Children appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Madhuku says police deployed 50 officers to block NCA meeting over CAB3 campaign @nehandaradio
item.description: Professor Lovemore Madhuku says police deployed more than 50 officers to prevent an NCA leadership meeting focused on opposing the recently enacted Constitution Amendment Act No. 6 of 2026, but claims the gathering went ahead at a secret venue. The post Madhuku says police deployed 50 officers to block NCA meeting over CAB3 campaign appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Zimbabwe Sees Record Growth in Mobile and Internet Access Despite Affordability Challenges
item.description: Zimbabwe Records Major Digital Growth, but High Internet and Smartphone Costs Remain a Challenge Zimbabwe has reached its highest levels of mobile phone ownership and internet connectivity, according to a new national survey. However, the high cost of smartphones and internet services continues to limit digital access for many people, particularly in rural areas. The [...] The post Zimbabwe Sees Record Growth in Mobile and Internet Access Despite Affordability Challenges appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Escalating Crackdown on Zimbabwe’s Opposition Amidst Constitutional Upheaval: CIOs Thoroughly Beat up MPs
item.description: HARARE — A chilling pattern of intimidation and violence is unfolding across Zimbabwe, casting a dark shadow over the nation’s political landscape. In a series of coordinated events that highlight the growing intolerance for dissent, opposition figures and civil society leaders are finding themselves in the crosshairs of suspected state security agents. The recent assault [...] The post Escalating Crackdown on Zimbabwe’s Opposition Amidst Constitutional Upheaval: CIOs Thoroughly Beat up MPs first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Thomas Mapfumo’s nephew defends Mukanya over US$1 million Chivayo deal @nehandaradio @wicknellchivayo
item.description: The national debate over Thomas Mapfumo’s decision to accept Wicknell Chivayo’s reported US$1 million performance package has intensified, with his nephew rallying behind the music icon while prominent lawyer Brighton Mutebuka argues the move has damaged his moral standing. The post Thomas Mapfumo’s nephew defends Mukanya over US$1 million Chivayo deal appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Zulu Monarch King Misuzulu Praises Mnangagwa’s Leadership Despite Worsening Citizens’ Suffering
item.description: Tinashe Sambiri| Zulu monarch King Misuzulu kaZwelithini has praised President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s leadership following talks in Zimbabwe, even as the... Continue reading "Zulu Monarch King Misuzulu Praises Mnangagwa’s Leadership Despite Worsening Citizens’ Suffering" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News
